paragraphs.body: Visualize, take a deep breathe, pay attention to the judge for the que to salute. Salute, do not forget to smile, start walking to the event, take a final deep breathe, get into your starting position, wait for your music to begin. The music starts, meaning you start performing the routine you have been practicing for months, remember every element, every toe point, jump, turn, relevé, all count towards your final score. The stress you had prior to starting is beginning to go away, you are finally getting into the groove of the routine fully embodying the music, the choreography flowing thoroughly. Finally, you finish the routine feeling pretty solid, you salute the judges, and sit there staring at the score board, the stress from before rushes over you once again, but worse. You begin to wonder why you do this to yourself, why you put yourself through this much stress just to receive a score based off of your performance, thinking about the amount of sacrifice it has taken you for this moment and the things you have learned, you realize and remind yourself that it is worth it. You close your eyes and just hope for a score of 9.5 or higher, it all sounds familiar right, the longing, the desire, the need to be perfect?

annotations.text: the longing, the desire, the need to be perfect?

annotations.comment: I'm still very taken with this descriptive introduction. It's great :)
annotations.context_left: to go away, you are finally getting into the groove of the routine fully embodying the music,

paragraphs.body: As you know, to live in the gymnastics gym growing up means knowing the strength, grit, and determination it takes to put everything into your sport. Growing up as a competitive gymnast means that I spent over twenty hours in the gymnastics gym every week, on school days from four pm to eight pm after school, to early morning and long days on the weekends, I was always in the gym. Being in the gym so much it forces you to grow up faster than the normal kid, forces you to be extremely determined, have perseverance, dedication, perfection, all of which I feel like I learned from the sport and use those skills everyday still. A big part of who I am is someone who is always trying their best, with something until the very end, and is able to go through hard things to get to the end goal. All of which was all fostered from the, minor, loss of my childhood to my sport.

annotations.text: Being in the gym so much it forces you to grow up faster than the normal kid, forces you to be extremely determined, have perseverance, dedication, perfection, all of which I feel like I learned from the sport and use those skills everyday still.
annotations.comment: Great ideas, but can you simplify this sentence? Perhaps break it up into two?
annotations.context_right: A big part of who I

paragraphs.body: Competitive gymnastics forces you to grow up faster than other kids your age because you are by yourself, completely intrinsically motivated, in practice at the ripe age of eight. You are held accountable for your reps, your performance, what you eat, how you take care of your body, at such an early age. Being determined is also a staple in gymnastics that has bled through to my everyday post-gymnastics life, it forces you to dive headfirst into something knowing that you will figure out how to get it done. Perseverance was a big thing I had to lean on, and still do, because it takes a lot of mental strength to continue with an extremely mentally and physically challenging sport like gymnastics. Perfection plagued me for a very long time, finally breaking out of the mold of being perfect, gymnastics forced you to be perfect, the sport is built around perfection. I gained the ability to always be dedicated in and to everything I do, that if I am going to do something then I will put my all into it until it is done to my standard, something you gain from the hours of repetition and skill work put into the gym.

annotations.context_left: out of the mold of being perfect, gymnastics forced you to be perfect, the sport is built around

paragraphs.body: Sometimes I used to feel like I was drowning in a sea of perfection Feeling like you are always seeking some sort of correction Feeling like it will be the end if unaccomplished Growing to learn everything isn’t meant to be so polished Coming to terms with the impossibility of perfection Perfection, an idea you are taught in and outside of the gym, you have a coach basically berating you to be nothing but perfect. Even the second you step outside of the gym you are molded and shaped by societal pressures to also be perfect, as a human and even more as a woman. Rather you do gymnastics, or not, everyone has felt the need to be perfect, the word floating through everyone’s mind. Perfect, practice make perfect, I must be perfect to be wanted, I need to be perfect to get what I want, to be liked, to be successful, all have been pushed and forced upon us from practically the day we began school. I am here to tell you, that this is not true in any means, I know, trust me, it is harder said than done, breaking the cycle of thoughts that live in your mind. However, you need to find something that will help you break through those thoughts, that will help you realize that you are in control on how you feel, and that perfection simply is not real. For me, it was visiting Maine, it seems like such a weird a minute trip, however, I can confidently say it changed me. Traveling up there made me realize how simple life can be, how I do not have to fit in with the crowd, party, go out, all things I hate. That I can live a simple slow, yet fulfilling, life, waking up and choosing to be happy and choosing to be kind every day. Slowly the thoughts of perfection started to become like small whispers in the back of my head, very unimportant and almost unable to hear.

annotations.context_left: Feeling like you are always seeking some sort of correction

paragraphs.body: Now, I know that this seems almost too simple, and I agree, I think honestly, I just got lucky, but that is something you have to do. Find something small, simple, and fulfilling in this world full of pressure and perfection. You must wake up and realize perfection is not attainable, because it simply does not exist. That you can try and waste your whole life trying to be someone or something that is just not possible to do, and you have to be okay with that. I know that is a big ask in this world, forgetting societal desires and foraging your own path and your own mindset, even though perfection and perfectness has always been, and will continue, to be pushed on to you. The moment you discover that you are in control of your life, how you feel, how you view yourself, and that the way you are, if it is true to who you are, is the best you can ever possibly get. Especially as a identifying woman or young girl, you are worth it, you are beautiful, you are amazing just the way you are, there is nothing better than just being yourself. Then I promise, life will be so much brighter, so much happier, and so much more satisfying.

annotations.text: The moment you discover that you are in control of your life, how you feel, how you view yourself, and that the way you are, if it is true to who you are, is the best you can ever possibly get.

paragraphs.body: Music starts, you have a smile on your face, you salute, you get out onto the floor feeling the music start, loving your choreography, nailing every element, like you practiced, with no care in the world other than to have fun and being proud of what you performed. Remember perfection may be asked of you, but if you give it your best and are loving it, that is as perfect as it can get. Now, rather you read this to remind yourself why you love gymnastics or trying to break the toxic cycle and mindset of perfection, no matter your decision or reasoning. Make sure the sport is still bringing you joy and is something fun that you look forward to before every practice and meet. And please always remember, your older gym sisters are always and forever there for you with whatever you need.

annotations.context_left: Music starts, you have a smile on your face, you salute, you get out onto the floor feeling
